success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-mode adapter strategy was appropriate for PyPSA (import feasibility 0.9, low intrusiveness risk).
Core MCP service startup/health checks passed (transport=stdio, service_health=healthy).
Repository structure and key modules were correctly identified (network, io, optimization, power_flow, statistics).
No runtime errors or warnings were reported by the pipeline.
failure_reasons: Quality gaps remain: generated metadata reports 0 LOC/0 tool_endpoints despite endpoint list existing, indicating instrumentation/reporting inconsistency.
Original project test result marked passed=false with empty details, so source baseline validation was not demonstrated.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external knowledge enrichment and confidence in automated documentation mapping.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; likely moderate overhead due to archive-based retrieval of ~301 files.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with broad package/dependency/module extraction; medium complexity repository analyzed adequately.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded and produced expected MCP scaffold files, but generated metrics (LOC/size/endpoints) appear incomplete.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health test passed quickly; however, functional test depth is low (no meaningful smoke/integration coverage captured).

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (reported 0 MB); likely acceptable for orchestration, but current metrics are not reliable.
